boilerplate.excerpt: Black, S. International Money Markets and Flexible Exchange Rates. Princeton Studies in International Finance, no. 32. Princeton, N.J.: Princeton Univ. Press, 1973.
boilerplate.reason: References section containing only citations without annotations or substantive content.

content_summary: This paper develops a macroeconomic theory of exchange rate determination under perfect capital mobility, showing how monetary expansions cause exchange rate overshooting due to the differential adjustment speeds between asset markets (fast) and goods markets (slow). The analysis demonstrates that rational expectations and structural parameters determine both the magnitude of overshooting and the adjustment path, with extensions showing how output responses can dampen exchange rate movements.
